shifts.trend: RPO $7.9B (Oct 31 2025) → $9.8B (Jan 31 2026, +24%); 46-48% to recognize next 12 months in both periods
shifts.note: RPO grew $1.9B in a single quarter — strong contract signing pace supporting forward revenue visibility

shifts.trend: Forbes Global 2000 customers ~43% of revenue at FY26; >$1M ARR customers 576 (FY25) → 733 (FY26, +27%)
shifts.note: Large customer cohort growing faster than total customers (+27% vs +21%) — upsell motion working; enterprise concentration is a feature, not a bug here

shifts.trend: Stock-based comp ~41% of revenue (FY25) → ~34% of revenue (FY26); operating cash flow $1.2B FY26
shifts.note: SBC declining as percentage of revenue — profitability trajectory improving even as growth held steady

shifts.trend: NRR 126% (FY25) → 125% (Q3 FY26) → 125% (FY26) — slight moderation holding stable
shifts.note: NRR stable in both periods; mgmt guides long-term decline as install base matures — the AI consumption uplift that would reverse this hasn't materialized yet

SNOW.verdict: Thesis intact but waiting for AI inflection — Snowflake's execution is clean and improving, but the inference-stack angle requires AI/ML workload consumption to drive NRR re-acceleration above 125%; that data point is absent from both filings

SNPS.headline: Ansys closed July 2025 and contributed $885.6M to Q1 FY26 revenue (+66% total), but Design IP declined -6% and China remained challenged; operating margin compressed by $394M quarterly amortization as $13.5B debt is aggressively paid down

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue $7.05B (+15%, $756.6M Ansys contribution); Q1 FY26 $2.41B (+66%, $885.6M Ansys); organic growth +10-15% in Design Automation ex-Ansys
shifts.note: Reported growth rate is Ansys-inflated; underlying organic Design Automation growth is healthy but harder to isolate post-merger

shifts.trend: FY25 China revenue -22% ex-Ansys on BIS export controls (May 2025 is-informed letter, rescinded July 2025); Q1 FY26 mgmt flags 'challenging near-term environment in China due to macro and Trade Restrictions'
shifts.note: China EDA restrictions created a one-time shock in FY25 Q3; risk remains elevated and mgmt not guiding China recovery in FY26

shifts.trend: Design IP segment FY25 weak (foundry softness + BIS + roadmap missteps); Q1 FY26 Design IP $407M (-6% YoY); mgmt guiding muted IP growth for full FY26
shifts.note: Design IP is the higher-margin business; its underperformance limits gross margin upside and is a headwind to the inference-chip design TAM thesis

shifts.trend: $13.5B total debt at FY25-end; Q1 FY26 paid off $3.5B term loan; $10B+ senior notes remain; buyback suspended pending debt paydown
shifts.note: Debt load from $34.9B Ansys deal is the dominant near-term capital allocation constraint; no buyback until leverage normalizes

shifts.trend: FY25 operating income $914.9M vs $1,355.7M prior (compressed by $457.8M Ansys amortization); Q1 FY26 operating income $203M vs $251.8M prior (compressed by $394.1M amortization + $118.3M restructuring)
shifts.note: Amortization drag will persist 10+ years; cash EBITDA is better than GAAP but the reported margin compression is real and structural near-term

SNPS.verdict: Thesis intact but impaired near-term — Synopsys holds the EDA choke point for AI chip design, but post-Ansys integration costs, Design IP weakness, and China restrictions create a 2-3 year period of margin compression before the strategic logic pays off

VRT.headline: Americas operating margin expanded from 26.8% (FY25) to 27.0% (Q1 FY26) as revenue grew from $10.23B (+27.7%) to a $2.65B quarterly pace (+30%), and gross margin improved 400bps to 37.7% — Vertiv is demonstrating operating leverage while backlog more than doubled to $15.0B

shifts.note: Americas growth rate accelerating sequentially; AI/HPC datacenter demand explicitly cited as driver in both filings

shifts.trend: Gross margin 36.3% (FY25, -30bps on tariffs) → 37.7% (Q1 FY26, +400bps YoY); Americas operating margin 26.8% (FY25) → 27.0% (Q1 FY26)
shifts.note: Gross margin expansion of 400bps QoQ is the single most important data point — confirms operating leverage is turning positive despite tariff headwinds

shifts.trend: Backlog $7.2B (YE24) → $15.0B (YE25, more than doubled) → customer orders placed 'in advance of our ability to fulfill them' per FY25 10-K; Q1 FY26 capex $114M on capacity expansion
shifts.note: Demand is running ahead of supply capacity — Vertiv is intentionally supply-constrained to avoid over-building; $425-525M CY26 capex is the response

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $226.4M; CY26 guided $425-525M (1.9-2.3x increase); Q1 FY26 $114M already on pace for guided range
shifts.note: Doubling capex to expand capacity across power, cooling, and HVDC architectures — multi-year investment consistent with $15B backlog

shifts.trend: Asia Pacific margin improving (11.0% FY25); EMEA $1.82B in FY25 (flat) with margin slipping; Q1 FY26 EMEA -20.3% — regional mix shifting to Americas
shifts.note: Americas is carrying the business while EMEA weakens; geographic concentration in Americas is both a strength (where hyperscaler DC spend is) and a risk

VRT.verdict: Thesis intact and highest-conviction — Vertiv's 400bps gross margin expansion while growing 30% confirms the operating leverage inflection; the doubled backlog and doubling capex commitment make VRT the clearest pure-play on the datacenter power and cooling bottleneck
